name: Fall Damage into Pit (2024 RAW: 1d6 per 10ft + prone)
description:
D&D 5e 2024 RAW: a creature that falls takes 1d6 bludgeoning per 10ft, max 20d6. A creature moving into a pit makes a DEX save (DC 10) to grab the edge; on fail, they take fall damage.

This scenario: Fighter moves into a 20ft-deep pit cell. Pit-terrain-resolver handles the DEX save and computes fall damage via computeFallDamage (domain/rules/combat-map-core.ts).

actions.comment: Place a 20ft-deep pit cell at (15, 10) — 5ft east of the fighter.

Walk into the pit cell. The pit-terrain-resolver uses its own seeded dice internally (hashStringToInt32 based on positions), so the outcome is deterministic-per-setup but NOT influenceable by queueDiceRolls. Test only validates the mechanic fires correctly.
Pit entry triggered. HP range covers both saved (54) and fell (as low as 34 for max 2d6 + some margin). Core validation: the pit resolver ran without error on movement into a pit cell.
